page: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It features a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional memory and multi-threaded performance. The server achieves top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 7394.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 464.59 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-core execution is highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Additionally, the instance delivers 131.7 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 124.08 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the m8g.16xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads due to its dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance is moderate, as shown by average stress-ng single-core scores. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs limits its suitability for heavy local disk-I/O or hardware-accelerated machine learning training. It is highly suited for memory-bound applications, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose, arm64-based server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance exhibits top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 464.59 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations. While single-core benchmarks are average, its dedicated core architecture delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. This server is optimized for multi-threaded applications, Redis databases, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.16xlarge is an arm64 server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores and 256 GB RAM, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core arm64 server delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Sixty-four dedicated arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
Two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of system memory with top-tier bandwidth
Thirty gigabit per second baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-threaded software compilation
Optimized for Redis, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference
